Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Dreadgods]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== Unsouled ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|'''Ch 20''' — The valley may need saving from the return of the Dreadgods.}}

== Skysworn ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 2''' — The Dreadgods watch the world.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 2''' — The Dreadgods are capable of executing a clan if they detect them.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 7''' — Dreadgods are disasters described as four monsters big enough to blot out the sky.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 7''' — Dreadgods are hungry for destruction and extremely powerful.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 7''' — None of the Dreadgods have ever been killed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 18''' — Dreadgods are believed to be responsible for the destruction of Sacred Valley.}}

== Ghostwater ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|'''Ch 19''' — The Dreadgods are beginning to act strangely.}}

== Underlord ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 5''' — Dreadgods are entities mentioned by Eithan.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 8''' — Eithan believes Dross will eventually dwarf Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 13''' — The rising of all four Dreadgods together previously resulted in the eradication of the previous generation of Monarchs.}}

== Uncrowned ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 4''' — The Dreadgods add uncertainty to the current balance.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 15''' — Dreadgods cause massive destruction.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 15''' — Monarchs have died trying to kill the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 15''' — Three Dreadgods woke to defend their brother during the Dread War.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|'''Ch 19''' — Monarchs control the entry of Dreadgods into Sacred Valley.}}

== Wintersteel ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 1''' — The stone panel in the labyrinth is carved with images of the four Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 4''' — Eithan pointed Tiberian toward the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 5''' — The Dreadgods are expected to invade the Blackflame Empire.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 8''' — Dreadgods are entities that could trample Blackflame City.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 8''' — Dreadgods are stirring.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 9''' — Dreadgods are after the labyrinth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 9''' — Dreadgods are capable of matching a Monarch for any period of time.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 9''' — Dreadgods satisfy their hunger while awake and wandering.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 11''' — The Dreadgods represent a danger to the Blackflame Empire.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 23''' — Dreadgods are beings that can wake up.}}

== Bloodline ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 4''' — Dreadgods are described as being only slightly less subtle than the heavens collapsing.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 21''' — One Monarch claims to have influence over the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 22''' — There is a method or knowledge regarding how to kill the Dreadgods.}}

== Reaper ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 2''' — The maze beneath them is the birthplace of the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 2''' — Dreadgods are anchored to life by something that, if removed, would make them mortal.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 2''' — There are five Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgods have been driven off for the moment.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — Attacks from Dreadgods or Monarchs have carved ravines into the territory around Refuge.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 5''' — The Dreadgods take the location of the Titan and Phoenix into account.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 9''' — The Dreadgods appear as a bird, a shelled warrior, a tiger with a halo, and a dragon accompanied by clouds.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 9''' — There is a way to kill the Dreadgods inside the labyrinth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 10''' — The door in the Ancestor's Tomb is marked with symbols of the four Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 10''' — Reigan Shen claimed he wanted to call the Dreadgods together to destroy them.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 11''' — The birthplace of the Dreadgods is located somewhere in the area.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 12''' — The Phoenix is made of liquid blood madra.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 13''' — Eithan theorizes that Dreadgods are the successful versions of dreadbeasts.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 16''' — Reigan Shen wants to bring the Dreadgods under control.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 17''' — No Monarch can defeat a Dreadgod individually.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 20''' — The Dreadgods are potential future opponents in the labyrinth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 22''' — Dreadgods are beings that the Monarchs sustain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 23''' — The origin of the Dreadgods is a topic discussed by Reigan Shen.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 25''' — Monarchs sustain the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 25''' — Monarchs maintain an equilibrium to prevent the Dreadgods from growing too strong.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 26''' — The Dreadgods are depicted as statues in the World of Night.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 27''' — The Dreadgods are stronger than ever.}}

== Dreadgod ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 2''' — The Dreadgods were all awakened on the day of the apocalyptic battle.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 2''' — Dreadgods represent a looming threat to Lindon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgods are still here.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 4''' — Lindon intends to research the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 4''' — Northstrider had a plan for the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 5''' — There is a relationship between Dreadgods and Monarchs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 5''' — The Dreadgods are entities that can be left behind.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 7''' — The Beast King is in a battle against the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 8''' — All four Dreadgods are awake at once.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 10''' — Northstrider is busy with the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 11''' — The presence of Monarchs on Cradle allows for the existence of the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 12''' — Lindon wants Mercy to help fight the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 12''' — The Dreadgods are the price paid to remain in Cradle.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 12''' — The Dreadgods are the consequence of the Monarchs upsetting a sort of balance.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 12''' — Lindon contemplates telling Mercy about the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 15''' — Orthos wants the Dreadgods to be dead.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 17''' — Redmoon sought to borrow the power of the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 19''' — Reigan Shen asked if Yerin felt proud of alienating allies before the Dreadgods arrive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 19''' — Dreadgods are after Lindon and Yerin.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 21''' — If a Dreadgod is killed, it draws the others and they all become stronger until the slain one is reborn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 21''' — Dreadgods have lived for thousands of years and are considered all but impossible to kill.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 22''' — The Dreadgods would go away if the Monarchs ascended.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 23''' — The Dreadgods are coming to Malice's territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 23''' — Lindon's goal is to kill the Dreadgods.}}

== Waybound ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 3''' — Lindon intends to prepare Orthos and Little Blue to fight the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 4''' — The Dreadgods are intelligent.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 7''' — The Dreadgods are not complete monsters like the Monarchs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 8''' — Yerin and Lindon are looking to get the Dreadgods out of Cradle.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 8''' — Yerin is planning on facing down both Dreadgods and Monarchs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 9''' — The empowered Dreadgods can move faster than ever.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 10''' — Lindon questions letting the Dreadgods live for centuries.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 12''' — The Dreadgods killed the Monarch who invented a certain technique.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 15''' — The Dreadgods are clashing in the sky.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 21''' — Dreadgods are too heavy to move and must use tricks like trading with something of equal weight to transport themselves.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 23''' — There are two Dreadgods left.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 23''' — The Dreadgods are currently rampaging.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 23''' — By killing the Dreadgods, Lindon put himself on a timer.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 24''' — Dreadgods are clever and have always possessed intelligence.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 24''' — Dreadgods are fundamentally made to devour the world.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 25''' — The intelligent population of the world could keep the Dreadgods fed with minimal loss of life.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 26''' — The Dreadgods loom large in the futures Malice glimpses.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 27''' — Monarchs can go toe-to-toe with Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 28''' — Malice allowed the Dreadgods to exist.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 29''' — The Dreadgods are closing in on Lindon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 30''' — The Dreadgods are destroying Reigan Shen's continent.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 30''' — Lindon's goal is to finish the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 33''' — Lindon lost consciousness while killing the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 35''' — The Dreadgods might be sealed in a labyrinth.}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 1''' — The Dreadgods caused untold devastation to the Blackflame Empire.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 3''' — Dreadgods possess immense power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 8''' — Some people come to the fighting arena to escape the Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 10''' — The enemy in the Iteration reminds Pride of the Dreadgods.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
